title: OFFSET without LIMIT causes error (stable/7)
description:
Compiled Virtuoso stable/7 branch on Ubuntu 13.04, cloned from Github yesterday.
Works:
SELECT * FROM <http://saffron.deri.ie/> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o }
LIMIT 1
OFFSET 2

Error: Virtuoso 22023 Error SR350: TOP parameter < 0
SELECT * FROM <http://saffron.deri.ie/> WHERE { ?s ?p ?o }
OFFSET 2

The error is the same one you get with LIMIT set to a negative number
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), value FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (2, 'test2', NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'test%' LIMIT 1 OFFSET 0;
